maml:description.maml:para: Add one or more IP rules to the Network ACLs of an Azure SignalR Service instance.

Adds IP rule entries (created with New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject) to the existing list of IP rules in the Network ACL configuration of an Azure SignalR Service. The cmdlet retrieves the current resource, appends the supplied rules, updates the service, and returns the updated Network ACL object. Duplicate rules are not filtered automatically; adding an identical rule will create another entry.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The SignalR service name.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
The SignalR service name.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Add IP rules using resource group & name (ResourceGroupParameterSet)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds two rules (an allow CIDR and a deny single IP) to the SignalR instance identified by resource group and name.

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Add IP rule using resource ID (ResourceIdParameterSet)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the SignalR instance, then adds a single allow rule using the resource ID parameter set.

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Add IP rules via pipeline SignalR object (InputObjectParameterSet)
command:example.dev:code: $ruleAllow = New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject -Value "40.4.0.0/24" -Action Allow $ruleDeny = New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject -Value "50.5.5.5" -Action Deny Get-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName "my-rg" -Name "mysignalr" | Add-AzSignalRNetworkIpRule -IpRule $ruleAllow,$ruleDeny

dev:remarks.maml:para: Pipes a SignalR resource object into the cmdlet and adds two IP rules.

maml:description.maml:para: Get a specific SignalR service or all the SignalR services in a resource group or a subscription.
Get a specific SignalR service or all the SignalR services in a resource group or a subscription.
SignalR service name.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Resource group name.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Resource group name.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR service resource ID.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
SignalR service name.
Resource group name.
The SignalR service resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 1: Get all SignalR services in the subscription ---
command:example.dev:code:
Get-AzSignalR
HostName Location ServerPort PublicPort ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- ---------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 5002 5001 Succeeded 1.0 mysignalr2.service.signalr.net eastus 5002 5001 Succeeded 1.0 mysignalr3.service.signalr.net eastus 5002 5001 Creating 1.0

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 2: Get all SignalR services in a resource group ---
command:example.dev:code:
Get-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup
HostName Location ServerPort PublicPort ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- ---------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 5002 5001 Succeeded 1.0 mysignalr2.service.signalr.net eastus 5002 5001 Succeeded 1.0

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 3: Get a specific SignalR service ----------
command:example.dev:code:
Get-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1
HostName Location ServerPort PublicPort ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- ---------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 5002 5001 Succeeded 1.0

command:example.maml:title: Example 4: Get a specific SignalR service from the default resource group
command:example.dev:code:
Get-AzSignalR -Name mysignalr2
HostName Location ServerPort PublicPort ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- ---------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr2.service.signalr.net eastus 5002 5001 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: The default resource group can be set by `Set-AzDefault -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup`.

maml:description.maml:para: Get custom certificate(s) for a SignalR service.
Retrieves custom certificates configured for a SignalR service. This can list all custom certificates or get a specific certificate by resource ID.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom certificate
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom certificate
The resource ID of a custom certificate
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom certificate
The SignalR resource object.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom certificate
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The resource ID of a custom certificate
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Get a custom certificate by resource group and SignalR service name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command get a custom certificate configured for the SignalR service "mySignalR".

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Get a custom certificate by a custom certificate resource ID

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command gets a specific custom certificate by its resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Get a custom certificate by SignalR resource object.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command get a custom certificate configured for the SignalR service represented by the `$signalr` object.

command:example.maml:title: Example 4: List all custom certificates for a SignalR service

dev:remarks.maml:para: Without specifying a certificate name, this command lists all custom certificates configured for the SignalR service "mySignalR".

maml:description.maml:para: Get custom domain(s) for a SignalR service.
Gets custom domains configured for a SignalR service. This can list all custom domains or get a specific domain.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom domain
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom domain
The resource ID of a custom domain
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom domain
The SignalR resource object.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the custom domain
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The resource ID of a custom domain
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Get a custom domain by resource group and SignalR service name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command get a custom domain configured for the SignalR service "mySignalR".

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Get a custom domain by a custom domain resource ID

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command gets a specific custom domain by its resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: -- Example 3: Get a custom domain by SignalR resource object. --

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command get a custom domain configured for the SignalR service represented by the `$signalr` object.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 4: List all custom domains for a SignalR service ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: Without specifying a domain name, this command lists all custom domains configured for the SignalR service "mySignalR".

maml:description.maml:para: Get the access keys of a SignalR service.
Get the access keys of a SignalR service.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
SignalR service name.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Resource group name.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR service resource ID.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
SignalR service name.
Resource group name.
The SignalR service resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 1: Get access keys of a specific SignalR service ---
command:example.dev:code:
Get-AzSignalRKey -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1
Name : mysignalr1 PrimaryKey : v******Ds= PrimaryConnectionString : Endpoint=https://mysignalr1.service.signalr.net;AccessKey=vmYRhoM62PMkNe/CSSPdMSxokn+WZEFmO QNt77PovDs=; SecondaryKey : 2******FQ= SecondaryConnectionString : Endpoint=https://mysignalr1.service.signalr.net;AccessKey=2+HkuxAA34xiZFFiDsVM0uDyzCsg6GKsd XSjN4C/YFQ=;

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Get access keys from a SignalR service object in pipe
command:example.dev:code:
Get-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 | Get-AzSignalRKey
Name : mysignalr1 PrimaryKey : v******Ds= PrimaryConnectionString : Endpoint=https://mysignalr1.service.signalr.net;AccessKey=vmYRhoM62PMkNe/CSSPdMSxokn+WZEFmO QNt77PovDs=; SecondaryKey : 2******FQ= SecondaryConnectionString : Endpoint=https://mysignalr1.service.signalr.net;AccessKey=2+HkuxAA34xiZFFiDsVM0uDyzCsg6GKsd XSjN4C/YFQ=;

maml:description.maml:para: Get SignalR replica resources for a SignalR service or a specific replica by name or resource ID.
Retrieves replica resources that belong to a SignalR service. You can: - List all replicas of a SignalR primary resource
- Get a specific replica by name
- Get a replica directly by providing its full resource ID
If no replica name is provided, all replicas for the specified SignalR service are returned.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the replica
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the replica
The SignalR resource object.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The resource ID of a replica
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The name of the replica
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The resource ID of a replica
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 1: List all replicas for a SignalR service ------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists every replica created for the primary SignalR resource "mySignalR".

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Get a specific replica by name ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the replica named "replica-westus2".

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 3: Use pipeline from a SignalR resource object ----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists all replicas for the SignalR resource represented by `$signalr`.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 4: Get a replica by resource ID -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the replica using its full ARM resource ID.

maml:description.maml:para: Get the usage quota of a subscription.
Get the usage quota of a subscription.
The SignalR service location.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR service location.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 1: Get the usage quota by inputting the location ---
command:example.dev:code:
Get-AzSignalRUsage eastus
Name CurrentValue Limit ---- ------------ ----- FreeTierInstances 2 5 SignalRTotalUnits 3 300

maml:description.maml:para: Create a SignalR service.
Create a SignalR service. The following values will be used for the parameters if not specified: * `ResourceGroupName`: the default resource group set by `Set-AzDefault -ResourceGroupName`.
* `Location`: the location of the resource group
* `Sku`: `Standard_S1`
The SignalR service name.
The allowed origins for the SignalR service. To allow all, use "*" and remove all other origins from the list. Slashes are not allowed as part of domain or after TLD
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable system-assigned identity for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service location. The resource group location will be used if not specified.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The service mode for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service SKU.
The tags for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service unit count, from 1 to 10. Default to 1.
The resource IDs of user-assigned identities to assign to the SignalR service.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The allowed origins for the SignalR service. To allow all, use "*" and remove all other origins from the list. Slashes are not allowed as part of domain or after TLD
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable system-assigned identity for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service location. The resource group location will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service name.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The service mode for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service SKU.
The tags for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service unit count, from 1 to 10. Default to 1.
The resource IDs of user-assigned identities to assign to the SignalR service.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: ------------- Example 1: Create a SignalR service -------------
command:example.dev:code:
New-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name mysignalr1 -Location eastus -Sku Standard_S1 -UnitCount 5
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 5 Succeeded 1.0

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 2: Specify ServiceMode and AllowedOrigin -------
command:example.dev:code:
New-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name mysignalr2 -Location eastus -ServiceMode Serverless -AllowedOrigin http://example1.com:12345, https://example2.cn
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Create a SignalR service with system-assigned managed identity
command:example.dev:code:
New-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name mysignalr3 -Location eastus -EnableSystemAssignedIdentity
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr3.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: The SignalR service is created with a system-assigned managed identity enabled. You can view the identity details in the `Identity` property of the output object.

command:example.maml:title: Example 4: Create a SignalR service with user-assigned managed identity
command:example.dev:code:
# First create a user-assigned managed identity $userIdentity = New-AzUserAssignedIdentity -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name myUserIdentity -Location eastus
# Create SignalR service with the user-assigned identity New-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name mysignalr4 -Location eastus -UserAssignedIdentity $userIdentity.Id
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr4.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: The SignalR service is created with the specified user-assigned managed identity. The identity can be used to access other Azure resources on behalf of the SignalR service.

command:example.maml:title: Example 5: Create a SignalR service with multiple user-assigned managed identities
command:example.dev:code:
# Create multiple user-assigned managed identities $identity1 = New-AzUserAssignedIdentity -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name myUserIdentity1 -Location eastus $identity2 = New-AzUserAssignedIdentity -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name myUserIdentity2 -Location eastus
# Create SignalR service with multiple user-assigned identities New-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name mysignalr5 -Location eastus -UserAssignedIdentity $identity1.Id, $identity2.Id
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr5.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: The SignalR service is created with multiple user-assigned managed identities. Note that SignalR does not support both system-assigned and user-assigned identities simultaneously.

maml:description.maml:para: Create a custom certificate reference for a SignalR service.
Creates a custom certificate reference for a SignalR service. The certificate is stored in Azure Key Vault and referenced by the SignalR service for custom domain configuration.
The custom certificate name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Base URI of the KeyVault that stores certificate.
Certificate secret name.
Certificate secret version.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The custom certificate name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Base URI of the KeyVault that stores certificate.
Certificate secret name.
Certificate secret version.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Base URI of the KeyVault that stores certificate.
Certificate secret name.
Certificate secret version.
The custom certificate name.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Create a custom certificate by resource group and SignalR service name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates a custom certificate named "myCertificate" for the SignalR service "mySignalR" using a certificate stored in Key Vault.

command:example.maml:title: - Example 2: Create a custom certificate with specific version -

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates a custom certificate with a specific certificate version from Key Vault.

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Create a custom certificate using SignalR resource object

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates a custom certificate using a SignalR resource object as input.

maml:description.maml:para: Create or replace a custom domain for a SignalR service.
Creates or replaces a custom domain for a SignalR service. A custom certificate must be configured before creating the custom domain.
The custom domain name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource id.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain value.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The custom domain name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource id.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain value.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource id.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain value.
The custom domain name.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Create a custom domain by resource group and SignalR service name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates a custom domain named "myDomain" for the SignalR service "mySignalR" using the specified certificate.

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Create a custom domain using SignalR resource object

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command creates a custom domain using a SignalR resource object as input.

maml:description.maml:para: Regenerate an access key for a SignalR service.
Regenerate an access key for a SignalR service.
The key type, either Primary or Secondary.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
Returns true if the regeneration was completed successfully.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
SignalR service name.
The key type, either Primary or Secondary.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true if the regeneration was completed successfully.
Resource group name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The key type, either Primary or Secondary.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true if the regeneration was completed successfully.
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
The key type, either Primary or Secondary.
SignalR service name.
Returns true if the regeneration was completed successfully.
Resource group name.
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 1: Regenerate the primary key ------------

maml:description.maml:para: Create an in-memory IP rule object for use with SignalR Network ACL Ip rule cmdlets.

Creates a PSIpRule object (not persisted in Azure) that defines a single IP rule entry with a value (single IP address, CIDR range, or Azure Service Tag) and an action (Allow or Deny). The resulting object can be supplied to Add-AzSignalRNetworkIpRule or Remove-AzSignalRNetworkIpRule.
Action for the IP rule. Allow or Deny. Default: Allow
IP rule value. Accepts IP, CIDR or ServiceTag.
Action for the IP rule. Allow or Deny. Default: Allow
IP rule value. Accepts IP, CIDR or ServiceTag.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 1: Create an allow rule for a CIDR range -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates an IP rule permitting traffic from the specified CIDR range.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 2: Create a deny rule for a single IP --------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates an IP rule that denies access for a specific IP address.

maml:description.maml:para: Create a new replica for an existing SignalR service.

Creates a new replica in a different region (or same region if supported) for a Premium tier SignalR service. Replicas are supported only for Premium SKUs (Premium_P1 or Premium_P2) and can be used for geo-distribution, disaster recovery, or traffic latency optimization.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The location where the replica will be created.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'. Default is 'Enabled'.
The resource group name.
Stop or start the resource. Valid values are 'true' and 'false'. Default is 'false'.
The SignalR service name.
The SKU of the SignalR replica. Default is Premium_P1. Only Premium_P1, Premium_P2 are supported.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The location where the replica will be created.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'. Default is 'Enabled'.
Stop or start the resource. Valid values are 'true' and 'false'. Default is 'false'.
The SignalR resource object.
The SKU of the SignalR replica. Default is Premium_P1. Only Premium_P1, Premium_P2 are supported.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The location where the replica will be created.
The replica name.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'. Default is 'Enabled'.
The resource group name.
Stop or start the resource. Valid values are 'true' and 'false'. Default is 'false'.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
The SKU of the SignalR replica. Default is Premium_P1. Only Premium_P1, Premium_P2 are supported.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 1: Create a replica in West US 2 -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a replica named "replica-westus2" in West US 2 with 2 units using Premium_P1.

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Create a replica using a SignalR object from the pipeline

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a replica in East US 2 by piping the primary SignalR resource.

maml:description.maml:para: Remove a custom certificate from a SignalR service.
Removes a custom certificate from a SignalR service. Note that you cannot remove a custom certificate that is currently being used by a custom domain.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR custom certificate resource object.
{{ Fill PassThru Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The custom certificate name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
{{ Fill PassThru Description }}
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The custom certificate name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
{{ Fill PassThru Description }}
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Remove a custom certificate by resource group name and SignalR service name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes the custom certificate named "myCertificate" from the SignalR service "mySignalR".

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Remove a custom certificate using certificate object

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes a custom certificate using an existing certificate object.

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 3: Remove a custom certificate by resource ID ----

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes a custom certificate by its resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: Example 4: Remove a custom certificate using SignalR resource object and certificate name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes a custom certificate from a SignalR service represented by the `$signalr` object.

maml:description.maml:para: Remove a custom domain from a SignalR service.
Removes a custom domain from a SignalR service. After removing the custom domain, clients will need to connect using the default Azure-provided domain.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR custom domain resource object.
{{ Fill PassThru Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The custom domain name.

The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
{{ Fill PassThru Description }}
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.

Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Remove a custom domain by resource group name and SignalR service name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes the custom domain named "myDomain" from the SignalR service "mySignalR".

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 2: Remove a custom domain using domain object ----

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes a custom domain using an existing domain object.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 3: Remove a custom domain by resource ID -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes a custom domain by its resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: Example 4: Remove a custom domain using SignalR resource object

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes a custom domain using a SignalR resource object as input.

maml:description.maml:para: Remove one or more IP rules from the Network ACLs of an Azure SignalR Service instance.

Removes the specified IP rule entries (created with New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject) from the existing list of IP rules in the Network ACL configuration of an Azure SignalR Service. Only exact matches (action and value) are removed.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The SignalR service name.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
IP rule object(s) created by New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject.
The SignalR service name.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Remove a rule using resource group & name (ResourceGroupParameterSet)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the allow rule for the given CIDR range from the specified SignalR instance.

command:example.maml:title: Example 2: Remove a rule using resource ID (ResourceIdParameterSet)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes a deny rule identified by value/action using the resource ID parameter set.

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Remove rules via pipeline SignalR object (InputObjectParameterSet)
command:example.dev:code: $rule1 = New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject -Value "30.3.3.3" -Action Allow $rule2 = New-AzSignalRNetworkIpRuleObject -Value "40.4.0.0/24" -Action Deny Get-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName "my-rg" -Name "mysignalr" | Remove-AzSignalRNetworkIpRule -IpRule $rule1,$rule2

dev:remarks.maml:para: Pipes the SignalR resource object and removes two IP rules at once.

maml:description.maml:para: Delete a SignalR replica resource.
Removes a specified replica from a SignalR service. This action is irreversible; the replica and its regional capacity are released. The primary SignalR resource remains unaffected.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The replica resource object.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource ID of a replica
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The replica resource object.
The replica name.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource group name.
The resource ID of a replica
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: ------------- Example 1: Remove a replica by name -------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Deletes the replica named "replica-westus2" and returns `True` on success.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Remove a replica by resource ID ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Deletes the specified replica using its full resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 3: Remove a replica from a replica object ------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Pipes the replica object into the removal cmdlet.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 4: Remove a replica from a SignalR object ------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the specified replica by piping the primary SignalR resource.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 4: Restart from a SignalR object -----------

maml:description.maml:para: Set the upstream settings of a SignalR service.
Set the upstream settings of a SignalR service.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
Clear all the upstream settings.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.

Template item(s) for upstream settings. Required key: UrlTemplate. Optional keys: HubPattern, EventPattern, CategoryPattern. Example using splatting syntax to pass templates parameter: @{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections1.com', HubPattern= 'chat';EventPattern='broadcast' },@{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections2.com'}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The SignalR service name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
Clear all the upstream settings.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.

Template item(s) for upstream settings. Required key: UrlTemplate. Optional keys: HubPattern, EventPattern, CategoryPattern. Example using splatting syntax to pass templates parameter: @{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections1.com', HubPattern= 'chat';EventPattern='broadcast' },@{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections2.com'}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
Clear all the upstream settings.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR service resource ID.

Template item(s) for upstream settings. Required key: UrlTemplate. Optional keys: HubPattern, EventPattern, CategoryPattern. Example using splatting syntax to pass templates parameter: @{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections1.com', HubPattern= 'chat';EventPattern='broadcast' },@{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections2.com'}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
Clear all the upstream settings.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR resource object.
The SignalR service name.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service resource ID.

Template item(s) for upstream settings. Required key: UrlTemplate. Optional keys: HubPattern, EventPattern, CategoryPattern. Example using splatting syntax to pass templates parameter: @{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections1.com', HubPattern= 'chat';EventPattern='broadcast' },@{UrlTemplate='http://host-connections2.com'}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 1: Set two ordered upstream templates --------

dev:remarks.maml:para: The following JSON represents the actual templates set.

` { "hubPattern": "chat", "eventPattern": "broadcast", "categoryPattern": "*", "urlTemplate": "http://host-connections1.com" }, { "hubPattern": "*", "eventPattern": "*", "categoryPattern": "*", "urlTemplate": "http://host-connections2.com" } `

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Clear all the upstream settings ----------

maml:navigationLink.maml:linkText: How to use splatting to pass parameters to commands in PowerShell

maml:description.maml:para: Start a stopped SignalR replica.
Make a replica resource active and starts serving traffic (if its endpoint is enabled). Use this after creating a replica in a stopped state or after maintenance.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR replica resource object.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.

Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.

The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource ID of the replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.

Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource group name.
The resource ID of the replica.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 1: Start a replica by name --------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Starts the replica named "replica-dr" and returns the updated replica object.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Start a replica by resource ID ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Starts the specified replica using its resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 3: Start a replica from a replica object -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Starts the replica provided through the pipeline.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 4: Start a replica from a SignalR object -------

maml:description.maml:para: Stop (disable) a running SignalR replica.

Sets `ResourceStopped` to `true` for a replica so it no longer serves traffic. Use this to temporarily remove a replica from rotation without deleting it (so it can later be restarted with Start-AzSignalRReplica).
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR replica resource object.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource ID of the replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR replica resource object.
The replica name.
Returns true when the command succeeds.
The resource group name.
The resource ID of the replica.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 1: Stop a replica by name --------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stops the replica named "replica-dr".

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 2: Stop a replica from a replica object -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stops the specified replica using pipeline input.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 3: Stop a replica by resource ID -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Stops the specified replica using its resource ID.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 4: Stop a replica from a SignalR resource object ---

maml:description.maml:para: Check whether a name is available. Alias: Test-AzSignal.
Check whether a name is available. If a name is available, returns true. Otherwise, return false. Alias: Test-AzSignal.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR service location.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR service location.
The SignalR service name.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: When the name is already in use, it's not available.
--- Example 2: When the name is not in use, it's available. ---

maml:description.maml:para: Update a SignalR service.
Update a SignalR service. The following values will be used for the parameters if not specified: * `ResourceGroupName`: the default resource group set by `Set-AzDefault -ResourceGroupName`.
* `Sku`: `Standard_S1`
The allowed origins for the SignalR service. To allow all, use "*" and remove all other origins from the list. Slashes are not allowed as part of domain or after TLD
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable or disable system-assigned identity. $true enables system-assigned identity, $false disables it. If not provided, no change happens on system-assigned identity.
The SignalR resource object.
The service mode for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service SKU. Default to "Standard_S1".
The tags for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service unit count, value only from {1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100}. Default to 1.
Set user-assigned identities. To remove all user-assigned identities, provide an empty array @(). If not provided, user-assigned identities remain unchanged.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The SignalR service name.
The allowed origins for the SignalR service. To allow all, use "*" and remove all other origins from the list. Slashes are not allowed as part of domain or after TLD
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable or disable system-assigned identity. $true enables system-assigned identity, $false disables it. If not provided, no change happens on system-assigned identity.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The service mode for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service SKU. Default to "Standard_S1".
The tags for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service unit count, value only from {1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100}. Default to 1.
Set user-assigned identities. To remove all user-assigned identities, provide an empty array @(). If not provided, user-assigned identities remain unchanged.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The allowed origins for the SignalR service. To allow all, use "*" and remove all other origins from the list. Slashes are not allowed as part of domain or after TLD
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable or disable system-assigned identity. $true enables system-assigned identity, $false disables it. If not provided, no change happens on system-assigned identity.
The SignalR service resource ID.
The service mode for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service SKU. Default to "Standard_S1".
The tags for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service unit count, value only from {1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100}. Default to 1.
Set user-assigned identities. To remove all user-assigned identities, provide an empty array @(). If not provided, user-assigned identities remain unchanged.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The allowed origins for the SignalR service. To allow all, use "*" and remove all other origins from the list. Slashes are not allowed as part of domain or after TLD
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable or disable system-assigned identity. $true enables system-assigned identity, $false disables it. If not provided, no change happens on system-assigned identity.
The SignalR resource object.
The SignalR service name.
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service resource ID.
The service mode for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service SKU. Default to "Standard_S1".
The tags for the SignalR service.
The SignalR service unit count, value only from {1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100}. Default to 1.
Set user-assigned identities. To remove all user-assigned identities, provide an empty array @(). If not provided, user-assigned identities remain unchanged.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 1: Update a specific SignalR service. --------
command:example.dev:code:
Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 -UnitCount 5
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 5 Succeeded 1.0

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 2: Specify ServiceMode and AllowedOrigin -------
command:example.dev:code:
Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup1 -Name mysignalr2 -ServiceMode Serverless -AllowedOrigin http://example1.com:12345, https://example2.cn
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 3: Enable system-assigned managed identity ------
command:example.dev:code:
Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 -EnableSystemAssignedIdentity $true
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command enables system-assigned managed identity for the SignalR service. The identity can be used to access other Azure resources on behalf of the SignalR service.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 4: Disable system-assigned managed identity -----
command:example.dev:code:
Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 -EnableSystemAssignedIdentity $false
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command disables system-assigned managed identity for the SignalR service.

command:example.maml:title: Example 5: Switch from system-assigned to user-assigned identity
command:example.dev:code:
# First create a user-assigned managed identity $userIdentity = New-AzUserAssignedIdentity -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name myUserIdentity -Location eastus
# Switch from system-assigned to user-assigned identity Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 -EnableSystemAssignedIdentity $false -UserAssignedIdentity $userIdentity.Id
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command disables system-assigned identity and enables user-assigned identity for the SignalR service. Note that SignalR does not support both system-assigned and user-assigned identities simultaneously.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 6: Update user-assigned identities ----------
command:example.dev:code:
# Create multiple user-assigned managed identities $identity1 = New-AzUserAssignedIdentity -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name myUserIdentity1 -Location eastus $identity2 = New-AzUserAssignedIdentity -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name myUserIdentity2 -Location eastus
# Update SignalR service with new user-assigned identities Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 -UserAssignedIdentity $identity1.Id, $identity2.Id
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command updates the SignalR service to use new user-assigned managed identities. Any existing user-assigned identities will be replaced with the specified ones.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 7: Remove all user-assigned identities --------
command:example.dev:code:
Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 -UserAssignedIdentity @()
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command removes all user-assigned managed identities from the SignalR service by providing an empty array.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 8: Completely disable managed identity --------
command:example.dev:code:
Update-AzSignalR -ResourceGroupName myResourceGroup -Name mysignalr1 -EnableSystemAssignedIdentity $false -UserAssignedIdentity @()
HostName Location ExternalIp Sku UnitCount ProvisioningState Version -------- -------- ---------- --- --------- ----------------- ------- mysignalr1.service.signalr.net eastus 52.179.3.5 Standard_S1 1 Succeeded 1.0

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command completely disables managed identity for the SignalR service by disabling system-assigned identity and removing all user-assigned identities.

maml:description.maml:para: Update a custom domain for a SignalR service.
Updates an existing custom domain for a SignalR service. This cmdlet allows you to modify the domain name or the associated custom certificate of an existing custom domain configuration.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource ID.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain name.
The SignalR custom domain resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The custom domain name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource ID.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain name.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The custom domain name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource ID.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain name.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource ID.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain name.
The resource ID of the custom domain.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The custom certificate resource ID.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The custom domain name.
The SignalR custom domain resource object.
The custom domain name.
The resource group name.
The resource ID of the custom domain.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Update the domain name of a custom domain by resource group and SignalR service name

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command updates the domain name of the custom domain "myDomain" for the SignalR service "mySignalR" to "newsignalr.contoso.com".

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 2: Update the certificate of a custom domain -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command updates the custom certificate used by the custom domain "myDomain" to a different certificate.

command:example.maml:title: Example 3: Update a custom domain using a SignalR resource object

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command updates a custom domain using a SignalR resource object as input.

command:example.maml:title: Example 4: Update a custom domain using the custom domain resource object

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command updates a custom domain by piping in the custom domain resource object.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 5: Update a custom domain by resource ID -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: This command updates a custom domain using its resource ID.

maml:description.maml:para: Update the Network ACL of a SignalR service.
Update the Network ACL of a SignalR service, including the default action, the network ACLs for public and private connection, and IP rules.
Allowed network ACLs
Run the cmdlet in background job.

Default Action of SignalR network ACLs, either allow or deny. It decides whether deny network ACLs or allow network ACLs take effect. For example, if the default action is allow, then only the deny ACLs matters.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Denied network ACLs
The SignalR resource object.
Name(s) of private endpoint(s) to be updated
Update public network ACLs
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The SignalR service name.
Allowed network ACLs
Run the cmdlet in background job.

Default Action of SignalR network ACLs, either allow or deny. It decides whether deny network ACLs or allow network ACLs take effect. For example, if the default action is allow, then only the deny ACLs matters.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Denied network ACLs
Name(s) of private endpoint(s) to be updated
Update public network ACLs
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Allowed network ACLs
Run the cmdlet in background job.

Default Action of SignalR network ACLs, either allow or deny. It decides whether deny network ACLs or allow network ACLs take effect. For example, if the default action is allow, then only the deny ACLs matters.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Denied network ACLs
Name(s) of private endpoint(s) to be updated
Update public network ACLs
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Allowed network ACLs
Run the cmdlet in background job.

Default Action of SignalR network ACLs, either allow or deny. It decides whether deny network ACLs or allow network ACLs take effect. For example, if the default action is allow, then only the deny ACLs matters.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Denied network ACLs
The SignalR resource object.
The SignalR service name.
Name(s) of private endpoint(s) to be updated
Update public network ACLs
The resource group name. The default one will be used if not specified.
The SignalR service resource ID.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: Example 1: Allow RESTAPI,ClientConnection for public network and set default action to Deny
Example 2: Allow client connection and server connection for a private endpoint connection
Example 3: Deny client connection for both public network and a private endpoint connection

maml:description.maml:para: Update properties of a SignalR replica.
Updates mutable properties on an existing replica including SKU, unit count (capacity), regional endpoint state, and tags. Only Premium SKUs are allowed.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR replica resource object.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'.
The SignalR replica service SKU.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'.
The resource group name.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR replica service SKU.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The replica name.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'.
The SignalR resource object.
The SignalR replica service SKU.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'.
The resource ID of the replica.
The SignalR replica service SKU.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Run the cmdlet in background job.
The credentials, account, tenant, and subscription used for communication with Azure.
The SignalR replica resource object.
The replica name.
Enable or disable the regional endpoint. Valid values are 'Enabled' and 'Disabled'.
The resource group name.
The resource ID of the replica.
The SignalR service name.
The SignalR resource object.
The SignalR replica service SKU.
The tags for the SignalR replica.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Scale a replica from 2 to 5 units ---------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Increases the capacity of the replica to 5 units.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 2: Disable the regional endpoint -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Temporarily disables the replica's endpoint.

command:example.maml:title: --------------- Example 3: Change SKU and scale ---------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Moves the replica to Premium_P2 SKU with capacity 200.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Update tags from a replica object ---------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds or updates tags on the replica.
